rev_clippy_ts_barber_shop_-27.5262_153.2.text:
Review 1. Megan Siebert, 5 stars, 5 months ago. Clippy T’s staff are professional and  friendly. Dan cuts my son Hugo’s hair and always makes him feel comfortable and works around his fear of having his hair cut.
I would give Clippy T’s a 6 star if possible.
They are fantastic.  Review 2. Padraig Gallagher, 1 stars, 6 months ago. Going to barber shop in late afternoon means U need a urgent cut, understand fully booked but understand first time customer no offer off first up appointment or service at all..
Tradies like myself would pay double for overtime..  Review 3. Ryan Bullard, 5 stars, 9 months ago. Got my haircut here before my wedding and couldn’t be happier with the clean fade and sharp beard trim. My barber was polite, chatty, and focused on every detail—even with a line of people waiting. Great experience, hope to be back soon!  Review 4. Andy Owens, 5 stars, a year ago. Not actually a customer as was just visiting the area however recently saw the staff at this barbershop assist a visually impaired man as he made his way to their shop. Extremely friendly and just seemed like genuinely good blokes.  Review 5. Chase Schneider, 5 stars, a year ago. Very clean shop and super friendly staff.
Music is not blaring and the lighting inside the shop doesnt blind you.
Iv had 3 haircuts and can confirm all staff are very skilled in barbering.
Staff are very relaxed and are willing to have a good chat or remain quite, whatever comforts you.

rev_cleveland_bowls_club_-27.5259_153.27.text:
Review 1. Susan Kelly, 5 stars, 3 months ago. New to Cleveland and thoroughly enjoyed the trivia night on Thursday ! Food was great ...tasty and good value  ! Definitely worth it  Review 2. Louise Cooke, 5 stars, 5 months ago. Great for special events.  Hosts/staff super helpful,  friendly and wonderful, doing everything to ensure the wedding was a success.  Great facilities catering to all your needs.  Thanks so much.  Review 3. Cathryn M, 3 stars, 10 months ago. Let me start by saying that we really wanted to love this place. We live a few minutes walk away and were talking about it maybe becoming our "local". I've been here a few times with my mum to listen to music and have a drink, but haven't had a meal for a few years. Today, three of us came for lunch. The place wasn't full, and the prices seem quite good (250gm steak is $29), so it had promise.

However, after 1hr20min, my 13yo son went to check on the food, as it had not arrived yet, and was told it would be another 20 mins. In other words, they hadn't even started cooking it. 1hr40min in, we got our meals. Children's burger, chicken burger, and 'medium' steak. The steak was closer to well done and basically cold; my teen said he cooked Woolies burgers at home the other week that were better (and he swears it was the same patties); and my partner said his burger was "okay" but the chips were good and crispy.

They say that out of quick, cheap, and good quality, you can only pick two. Unfortunately, the food was fairly cheap, and that's it.

The wait service staff were lovely and apologetic for the wait, but they should tell you when you order that it will take close to 2 hours to get your meal. I was having a drink and enjoying the atmosphere, which does have that nice, relaxed, old school feel... But my 2 lunch companions had both arrived hungry, which didn't help matters at all!

Very disappointing overall. Almost gave 2 stars, but the prices and relaxed, overall feel of the place gave it 1 more. Would come here for a drink maybe, but we have decided to try another local.  Review 4. Colin Prinsloo, 4 stars, 2 years ago. 3x greens, pokies and a large lounge and dinning area. Friendly service.  Review 5. Nicholas Sindici, 5 stars, 3 years ago. In the drag show night we had the set menu and was fabulous

rev_the_select_inn_ryde_-33.8066_151.073.text:
Review 1. DALE WELLHAM, 4 stars, a week ago. Great location for attending a concert at Sydney Olympic Park. The gentleman at the front desk was very pleasant and helpful, which made check-in/check-out easy. The included continental breakfast was also quite good. Overall, a very enjoyable stay.  Review 2. Chris Hodder, 5 stars, a month ago. I travel to Sydney every six weeks for medical appointments at Ryde Hospital, and this motel has become my go-to place to stay. It’s only about a 10-minute drive to the hospital, which makes those early mornings a lot less stressful.
The beds are incredibly comfortable, and the rooms are always clean and well-maintained. They have pretty much everything you need for a comfortable stay; the only thing missing is a microwave, which is the only reason I gave the room four stars instead of five.
The staff are consistently friendly and genuinely make an effort to ensure your stay is a good one. A special mention to Amy at reception, she’s a lovely lady who always takes the time to help whenever needed, which really makes a difference when you’re travelling for medical reasons.
There’s plenty of parking available. It can be a little tight at times, but it’s free and does the job. A big bonus, unlike many motels in Sydney, the parking is free.
Overall, it’s a great motel in a location that perfectly suits my needs, and I’m always happy to return.  Review 3. James Russell, 5 stars, a month ago. We would like to say we were pleasantly surprised at the cleanliness, comfort, quietness and the pleasant view of the golf course. What really impressed us is that we had a funeral to attend at the golf club at midday and it it wasn’t an issue at all getting into our room early to get dressed as we had flown in from interstate earlier in the morning. Thank you very much for the great service and room.  Review 4. Tina g, 5 stars, a month ago. We had a lovely stay, the room was very clean and tidy it was lovely to see that they have renovated the room and bathroom. The continental breakfast was nice it was a great way to start your day and if you book direct through them it is free. It was also nice to watch the people playing golf. We had a little problem with the TV channels, but they came straight away and fixed the issue. It was also a great price and to also receive breakfast was a big bonus it was very affordable to stay there.  Review 5. Kristy ROBB, 5 stars, 4 months ago. My son and I loved our room. Very clean and it felt quite luxurious for the small price tag. The view to the golf course was great, parking just outside our door and quieter than I expected.
We were looked after with 2 beds also which was even more comfortable. Love the modern bathroom and rain shower with fluffy towels.
We will definitely stay again.

rev_comfort_hotel_-27.5255_153.2704.text: Review 1. LISA WILLIAMS, 1 stars, 2 months ago. The place is awful. It’s overpriced for what you get even without all of the issues that I had. The reception and cleaning staff were lovely, but the manager was rude and had terrible communication skills. The highlight was the homeless man fully clothed in the pool exfoliating his feet. There is no security. It’s unsafe and I will be reporting it to the relevant authority.  Review 2. Braddo, 1 stars, 3 months ago. Would have to be in the top 5 worst places I have ever stayed at. Bloke at reception can’t speak English. No maintenance whatsoever! The place is falling apart. They must have a deal with the government for cheap emergency accommodation for people on the dole as the car park was used by what seemed to be less fortunate people as a drinking spot. I would recommend keeping an eye on your children. There was a crack in the bathtub, carpet ripped, beds are stuffed. The fridge was super loud and I had to turn it off to be able to sleep. Too many things to mention. I would not recommend this place to anyone. If you are looking for an insurance claim, this is the place for you as there are slip, trip and fall hazards everywhere.  Review 3. Rozz Parsons, 1 stars, 4 months ago. Where to begin..this place is a bump. Rooms are big but grotty and grubby. Bathroom fixtures have been changed and don't match the old footprint. Bottles of some sort of wash missing but holder still there and the bathroom door was broken. Can't use the elevator out of office hours, so we had to carry heavy bags down the stairs. We were phoned by the property and accused by some rude woman of having a cat in the room, no apologies came forth when they were told it wasn't us. Rubbish in the hallways and walkways for days. The pool looked good and the place  has good dining options within walking distance. Cannot recommend this place at all.  Review 4. Tracy Oshea, 3 stars, 4 months ago. Average hotel rooms, old ,clean. Friendly staff  but lack in people skills and service. Over priced for what it is. Make sure you have personal things hidden as manager takes it upon herself to enter rooms and go through belongings and open things she has no right too. Total breach of privacy. The location couldn't be better. Everything is within walking distance. They have rooms with disabled showers. Toaster, kettle, tea and coffee etc. And a small bar fridge. Woolworths, Coles, doctors etc across the road as well as eat-in or take-away shops. You can take an evening stroll at Raby Bay waterfront or eat at the many lovely seaside restaurants. Location, location location. Unfortunately i would never recommend or stay here again. A lot of yelling and swearing from homeless people and reception allows pot smoking for some and turns and bends rules for others to what suits her at the time. Also talks about guests with other guests and I know this first hand from experiencing it with her. A very false sense of security.  Review 5. Michelle Brown, 1 stars, 5 months ago. Booked 2 rooms.. Absolutely discussing..  dirty towels on the back of bathroom door. Foot marks in shower hair on basin. Air con didn't work in one room. When booking was not told the pool was green. I had 3 teenagers with me. My daughter lifted her pillow and found bugs. The smell of vomit in hallway was absolutely discussing. Finished my engagement at 11.30 to return to motel. The smell was so bad we returned to home at 1am. 3 hours away. When ringing to complain was spoken to like it didn't matter. I work hard for my money and for nearly $500.  Don't stay

rev_the_sebel_sydney_martin_place_-33.86.text:
Review 1. Noela Foote, 5 stars, 2 weeks ago. Well as an avid and regular  traveller this hotel  has to be up there as one that is soooo very good!   The staff were really friendly and helpful.  The facilities in the room were great including a fridge packed of cocktails, wines, beer and soft drinks!   There was also a microwave and a sink!  Its location is fantastic and close to everything you need in terms of light rail, buses, and airport trains!
Bring an Accor member I was given a lovely small bottle of Prosecco upon arrival.
Whilst they don’t have an internal restaurant there is a fabulous restaurant across the road called La Riviera!   I will stay here every time I visit Sydney!   Thank you Accor!  🙏👍👏  Review 2. Hollie Lagerberg, 3 stars, 3 weeks ago. The hotel is in a good location.  They DO NOT have a restaurant on-site.  I would not recommend getting the breakfast option when booking the room - you are best walking across the road or around the corner to get your own breakfast which will have more options and less cost.
The rooms are not large.  Cleanliness in my room was acceptable but not great. I saw other guests incredibly upset at the state of their rooms.
There was load construction noise until late at night.
I paid $1,794.55 for 3 nights which I think is massively excessive for what I got.
I would not stay here again.
The staff, however, were lovely.  Review 3. Elayne Baker, 4 stars, a month ago. Clean and comfortable. Everything worked well. Shower was very nice. Bed was a bit stiff, they added an extra mattress pad which helped but a bit more was needed for my comfort. Otherwise it was a lovely hotel, centrally located with a lovely breakfast place across the street.  Review 4. Kate Flood, 3 stars, 2 months ago. Amazing concierge service when checking in to receive an upgraded style room  & store luggage  as we were off to a post Xmas function & in a hurry …….
When returning just before midnight to find our room with long black hairs all over bathroom floor - obviously not cleaned at all -too late to complain - but felt “ icky” about it .
Informed concierge when checking out this morning - she was really not very apologetic at all & said she would check in with housekeeping

Never ever had this happen at any Sebel hotel prior - a bit shocking to be honest !!!  Review 5. Chantelle Parker, 4 stars, 3 months ago. In comparison to the luxury of other Sebels, the level of luxury was lacking.

The breakfast is off site at a small hole-in-the-wall cafe. I paid extra for the included breakfast but turns out… I would have saved more money if I ordered the same thing and paid for it myself. My breakfast ticket said “juice and coffee” and it should have read juice OR coffee. You can NOT order it to your room despite the website stating the cafe does room service.

Ah the pool! The pool is offsite too… and they charge you too. You also have to lug your Sebel-branded pool gear from the hotel to the pool. We skipped the pool, but not for lack of want. A 5 min jump in and out wasn’t viable for us.

The room was small but comfortable with good facilities. The shower was dripping so we had to keep the bathroom door shut

The room was quiet which was good. The body wash smelled lovely too.

$10 for some pringles is a bit much. We didn’t use the mini bar purely based on pricing.

Location fabulous. Walking or metro to anywhere basically.

Anyway, in summary… did the job, felt clean.

Money grab for sure.

Enjoy your stay.

rev_st_james_hotel_-33.8711_151.2096.text:
Review 1. ken stha, 5 stars, a month ago. This is my regular coffee place, and I already love coming here but today was something truly different.

My coffee was so beautifully presented that I was genuinely mesmerised. It was just next level. I couldn’t stop admiring it. The attention to detail in the presentation made it feel extra special, and I’m honestly obsessed with the way it was served.

It’s usually quite busy and the staff are often rushing (which is totally understandable), but today everything felt elevated. And the cappuccino…!! Absolutely perfect — smooth, rich, and heavenly.

A big 5 stars from me ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐  Review 2. Christopher Jones, 5 stars, 6 months ago. 10/10 as a Father's Day lunch experience. Located at the St James end of Hyde Park and perfect for outdoor dining. Every dish was generously sized and very tasty, especially the boscaiola with truffle.
They have two great exclusive beers on tap, I particularly enjoyed the St. James XPA. The staff are very friendly and make the experience even better. Keep up the great work guys, we will be back  Review 3. Nisa Chinhango, 5 stars, 9 months ago. This place has a great atmosphere and the staff are incredibly friendly. It’s right next to a garden, so the view of the greenery is really soothing. The music is lovely, and every dish is delicious. A special shoutout to the mushroom soup it’s absolutely divine! 🍄🌿  Review 4. GA YA, 5 stars, 11 months ago. We went the the first time just for coffee. We came back the next day for breakfast. The setting in Hyde park itself is amazing. We were very surprised at the variety of foods on offer. A bit pricy but justified by high qualified products. Very friendly and efficient staff. We loved it ♥️  Review 5. Annette Mavin, 5 stars, a year ago. Delicious food in a beautiful outdoor setting in the heart of the city. Service is quick and food serving was generous. Quite a variety of food and drinks available at reasonable prices. Coffee was delicious, in fact I had a second cup. Ample seating outdoors and covered by umbrellas. Lovely cafe..,

rev_qt_sydney_-33.8709_151.2077.text:
Review 1. Peter Baron, 4 stars, 2 weeks ago. QT hotel has a great location in the city. Very comfortable bed and the room was very quiet so sleeping was a breeze. The staff were very friendly and helpful making my stay experience really pleasant. The bar and restaurant Gowings was awesome really well set out great service in a relaxed atmosphere. Great little cafe downstairs named Parlour. Good coffee and a lovely breakfast before heading out for the day. Overall really enjoyed my stay at QT hotel.  Review 2. Kim A, 5 stars, 3 weeks ago. We enjoyed a 3 night stay at QT hotel for our  anniversary. The staff graciously upgraded our room and sent up a bottle of prosecco for us to enjoy which we did!  The hotel was in the perfect location with transit close by to get wherever you wanted to go.  We had breakfast in the restaurant both mornings and was delicious. Very helpful staff  Review 3. L L, 3 stars, a month ago. Great location (in the middle of a shopping mall). Fabulous interior design (Luigi Manson 2 style). Comfortable bed. Nice bath and great bathtub. Spacious.
However,
Staff did not provide information on where the facilities are on arrival. The TV home button was broken, so we could not find any information about the hotel facilities. The TV button was repaired on our 3rd day of stay.
For a family with children, the external swimming pool is only for those over 18.
For business travel, there is no dry cleaning service on Sundays, so you need to handle it yourself.  Review 4. Chris Stewart, 4 stars, 2 months ago. Staff are very friendly. Decor is quirky and fun. Parlour a nice place for a drink, breakfast was ok. (gets busy). Gowings bar and grill very good. Fantastic location on Market St. Rooms just a little dark (esp in bathroom). Super quiet with double glazed windows👍🏻We had a lovely two night stay. Thanks  Review 5. Frank DiGiuseppe, 5 stars, 3 months ago. Spent 3 very pleasant nights here. Room was quiet, clean, comfortable and the AC worked well (and quietly! We like it cool to sleep). Included breakfast was very good, with a good selection of dishes (NOT a buffet). And front desk service was very friendly and helpful. And to top it off, the location was ideal! Very close to lots of shopping (including the QVB), a couple museums, and a 10 min tram ride to the harbor area (the Rocks, Opera House, and royal botanical garden).
